bullets.text: Q2 CR 96.8; YTD through June 95.7 — on track for 96 annual goal despite extreme weather; April CR 98.4 followed by May and June at or below 96; absorbed heavy catastrophe volume while maintaining annual profitability trajectory.

NPW grew 13% in Q2; net earned premium grew 11%; Agency auto PIFs up 4% (significant recovery from prior weakness); Direct auto and Commercial Lines each at 11% PIF growth; special lines growing despite high market share and sector headwinds.

Personal auto average premium per policy up ~4% YOY; commercial auto average premium up ~13%; retention improving as healthier customer mix and retention initiatives take hold; Robinson (auto+home bundle) beginning a positive growth trend toward end of quarter.

Progressive capital position strong; ASI transactions optimized risk and capital structure — exited commercial property business and terminated quota share reinsurance (one-time NPW boost); ASI residential homeowners risk now contributing fully to Progressive consolidated results.

Extreme weather events flowed continuously from Q1 into Q2 creating 'rope-a-dope' conditions; catastrophes not classic-declared but still significantly impacted monthly CRs; Commercial Lines saw rare frequency spike raising questions being closely monitored.

Commercial Lines frequency spike addressed with rate and underwriting actions; no systemic loss recognition miss identified; annual policy renewal cadence means rate increases take time to earn through; management monitoring carefully.

Snapshot UBI program growing; ASI Property integration enabling bundled Robinson growth; Progressive Advantage Agency expanding capabilities; Investor Relations meeting in October to showcase Destination Era progress including Snapshot and customer marketing/retention deep dives.

Agency auto PIF growth of 4% significant relative to multi-year weakness in channel; Robinson bundled growth driven by both Agency and Direct; Platinum agent program building momentum; Direct channel showing sustained double-digit growth in core metrics.

Final shareholder letter from Glenn Renwick after 16.5 years reporting to shareholders; Tricia Griffith assumed CEO letter responsibilities in Q3; Glenn described Progressive as 'a high-energy time at our $20 billion plus scale.'

Double-digit growth at $20B+ scale unprecedented in company history; catastrophe response capabilities validated across multiple large events; fast-growth management challenge acknowledged — maintaining staffing quality and brand integrity during rapid expansion.
